attacking_style: Long-range diesel attacks on major climbs (e.g., 6-minute solo advantage on Alpe d'Huez), but generally passive in select groups within striking distance. Strong descending acceleration when supporting leaders.
team_tactics: Primary role as high-altitude domestique for Jumbo-Visma leaders, pacing flat TT sections and chasing efforts. Team leverages his ITT strength in stage races but rarely gives full GC freedom.
terrain_preference: Long, sustained climbs where diesel engine excels; strong ITT capabilities allow GC gains on flat time trials. Struggles with technical finishes, altitude power limitations, and explosive finale climbs.
vulnerability: Exposed in echelon situations and wind splits due to positioning weaknesses. Durability fades after extended domestique work in third week. Technical bike handling under pressure repeatedly costs time.
key_patterns: Repeatedly dropped on climbs after extended domestique duties, showing durability limits in Grand Tour third weeks (3 instances)
Strong ITT performances claw back 30-75 seconds regularly, jumping GC positions in stage race tests (3 instances)
Positioning errors in echelons and light-wind situations isolate him from protected groups (2 instances)
Flat TT advantages evaporate on altitude climbs, revealing sustained high-altitude power ceiling (2 instances)
Technical bike handling issues cost significant time in pressure moments, especially TT finishes (2 instances)
form_trajectory: negative: Recent results show declining ability to sustain GC efforts—lost contact early 2022 Romandie climb, dropped unexpectedly 2021 Dauphiné, multiple positioning failures 2021-2022 suggest reduced competitive edge
